crime: The one examination her whole future sat on — she had prepared past anyone, and on that day she was not good enough. The life she drilled for went to other girls; since then, everything the prison admires in her is a second try, and no one has ever seen her first.

At breakfast the games-mistress announced a mock examination for all the girls — one sitting, sealed papers no one else would see, no retakes. She was conscripted as helper and barred from the papers.

The games-mistress kept a sleeping draught dispensed from the medical room, bottled in her cell; she portioned the week's supply in advance into folded papers — one dose per evening. With the cells open each afternoon and her errands as helper for cover, she thinned the bottle a night's measure at a time.

Her first reach knocked the row; a girl caught her at the shelf and teased her. Days later, one of the games-mistress's folded papers was found sprung, powder in the pocket seam; the games-mistress re-portioned it and joked the bottle was running low — sleeping worse than she thought.

On the eve of the examination, she packed every measure she had taken that week into a single paper and refolded it to the games-mistress's own crease. She spent the evening in the lounge, in sight of everyone.

The games-mistress took her paper that night and did not wake. Found at morning unlock — the bottle low beside six honest papers. What it looked like: a girl who had been over-drinking her sleep for days.

The keeper of the medical room filled the bottle to the mark on its set day; the nights since left a week's measure unaccounted, and the six remaining papers each held the true dose — the excess had never passed the games-mistress's own hand. A week thinned from the bottle and returned in a single paper, by a hand at the shelf twice, fumbling once each time and never again. For the girl no one has ever seen fail twice, a single fumble is a signature.
fields.epitaph: She could undo every error she ever made — the true examinations of a life are sat once.
fields.epithet: She Whose Firsts All Happened in the Dark
execution: The execution stand rose as an examination hall set for one: her mark behind a shut gate, the course beyond it in plain view.
Above the gate, a start-bell with its hammer raised; beside her, a water-clock draining in full view — when the last of the water runs out, the floor beneath her mark opens.
She took her mark and set her stance, eyes on the hammer, and held ready the whole allotted hour. The hammer never fell.
The clock ran dry, the bell hung unrung, and the floor at the mark let go.

magic.name: Never Twice
magic.text: Once her body has truly failed at an act, she performs it without error from then on.
